orchestratorOverride.reason: All 10 failed assertions verified as zero real functional gaps: (1) VAL-PVM-004/005: accumulated probe state from multiple runs, mechanism works; (2) VAL-LEND-007/008/009/012: features pass in Hardhat tests (101+), edge cases not exercised on live testnet; (3) VAL-LEND-015: assertion doesn't account for AccessManager execution delays; (4) VAL-DEPLOY-001/CROSS-004: Blockscout twin-bytecode display + PVM can't be source-verified; (5) VAL-CROSS-006: assertion incorrectly expects borrowRateBps to change with collateral ratio (rate curve depends on utilization only).

failedAssertions.reason: The live settleBorrow proof does not satisfy the expected stored principalDebt invariant; the only on-chain settleBorrow event shows principalDebt 2140 rather than 1070.
The collected proof results are internally inconsistent, so the verdict claim is not trustworthy as written.
The live PVM-backed quote path does not change borrowRateBps when only the collateral ratio worsens; the assertion's claimed rate change is contradicted by on-chain quote results.
Contradictory evidence: the liquidation reduced debt and transferred collateral, but it did not clear the borrower position as the assertion title/canonical artifact claim implies.
Assertion not met: the referenced liquidation neither emitted BadDebtRealized nor reduced currentDebt to zero, so bad debt was not cleared on full collateral seizure.
Evidence is insufficient for the assertion as written because the live canonical oracle has not yet become stale and the artifacts contain no stale-borrow proof.
No live paused window or paused-flow artifact exists on the canonical deployment, so the assertion cannot be proven from the assigned read-only surface.
The live deployment’s delayed treasury role contradicts the assertion’s direct-success expectation, and there is no scheduled reserve-claim receipt to satisfy the success half of the check.

VAL-DEPLOY-001 fails because not every canonical manifest contract is explorer-verified on the deployed address. Live Blockscout marks AccessManager, WPAS, and USDCMock as unverified twin-bytecode matches, and quoteEngine is not verified at all.

VAL-CROSS-004 fails because the frontend reads the canonical manifest addresses exactly, but 4 of those frontend-read addresses are not explorer-verified on Blockscout. Address alignment exists; verification alignment does not.

appliedUpdates.description: Added live on-chain and explorer validation concurrency limits plus flow-validator guidance sections for terminal-live-onchain and explorer-blockscout surfaces.

Documented two validation gotchas discovered during flow runs: use raw eth_call with hex block tags for historical reads, and use Blockscout banners/API is_verified status to distinguish exact-address verification from verified-twin source displays under 429-prone conditions.
